biography: Florence Nightingale was a British social reformer, statistician, and founder of modern nursing. During the Crimean War she reduced hospital death rates from 42% to 2% through sanitation reforms. She was also a pioneer of data visualization, using her 'coxcomb' charts to convince policymakers of the importance of hygiene.
aiSummary: Florence Nightingale was the founder of modern nursing who reduced Crimean War hospital deaths from 42% to 2%. Pioneer of medical statistics and data visualization. Based in London, United Kingdom.
techniquesInvented: Polar area diagram (coxcomb chart) for medical statistics

timeline.title: Led nursing team in Crimean War
timeline.description: Organized and reformed military hospital nursing at Scutari, dramatically reducing mortality

timeline.title: Published statistical analyses
timeline.description: Used pioneering data visualizations to demonstrate preventable deaths from poor sanitation

timeline.title: Founded Nightingale Training School
timeline.description: Established the first secular nursing school at St Thomas' Hospital, London

timeline.title: Order of Merit
timeline.description: First woman to receive the Order of Merit from King Edward VII
